key_issues: NON-BLOCKING (parity): spec marks all request-params structs '(loose)' but schemas.ts shows BaseRequestParamsSchema = z.object (NOT loose) — concrete request params (InitializeRequestParams etc.) STRIP unknown non-_meta keys rather than preserve them. Only ResultSchema/GetTaskPayloadResult/capability trees are truly looseObject. _meta is a named field so _meta passthrough is fine either way. Fold a chosen option into Decisions; scope the 'extra inner key preserved' assertion to results only, not request params.

NON-BLOCKING (test coverage): Part-1 fixtures cover envelope kinds but not every discriminated-union arm. Make 'one round-trip fixture per union arm' a hard minimum: 5 ContentBlock, 5 SamplingMessageContentBlock (incl. toolUse/toolResult, which never appear in the general ContentBlock test), 5 EnumSchema, 2 ElicitRequestParams, 2 ResourceContents, 4 PrimitiveSchema, plus a populated NESTED capability (current cap test is shallow: roots.listChanged only).

NON-BLOCKING (anti-vacuous-pass): hand-authored fixture + hand-authored struct by same author can agree on a wrong camelCase key and still round-trip green. Add a field-mapping unit test (kebab<->camel: $schema, _meta, serverInfo, inputSchema) INDEPENDENT of fixtures, and require fixture camelCase keys be copied from the TS interface, not retyped.

COSMETIC: result count is exactly 18 (the 17 in Expected-Outcomes list + CreateTaskResult, line 1396 'extends Result', present in inventory section J but omitted from the Expected-Outcomes results bullet). Also '16 client + 8 server = 18 distinct' arithmetic is confusing; restate as '20 distinct request structs (16 client union 8 server, 4 shared: ping + 4 task requests)'.

ADVISORY: split progress.md:47 (currently bundles spec-2025-11-25.rkt + spec-2026-07-28.rkt on one row) into two deliverable rows so this item's half can advance independently of item 004 — Completion Reminder already mandates not flipping the combined row; splitting is cleaner.

No missing types.

fixture_plan: ADEQUATE — correctly rejects specTypeSchema.examples.ts (JSDoc bait) and the static type-assignability test as fixture sources (verified no *.json fixtures exist); hand-authoring with contract-validation as the honesty safeguard is the right call. Reinforce per union-arm coverage and an independent field-mapping test.
